question: Is the spelling of the center cover text exactly "May you walk through storms and still be kind, may you always find light above and a path beneath your feet"?
Is the center text rendered as cut-out text with slightly fuzzy but neat edges?
Is the cut-out text positioned at the center of the card cover?

Is the spelling of the text on the narrow paper strip exactly "Though miles may lie between us, we are never far apart; the road ahead is long, so write often and don't hold your thoughts inside too long"?
Is the text on the narrow paper strip rendered in a typewriter font?
Is the typewriter text located on a narrow paper strip on the right side of the inside page?
Is the spelling of the text on the beige ribbon exactly "Write your worries on paper, fold them away for tomorrow to handle, and sleep peacefully tonight"?
Is the ribbon text rendered as fine black text on a beige paper ribbon?
Is the beige ribbon with text tied at the bottom of the inside page?
Is the spelling of the text on the wax seal exactly "May peace and joy be yours, may your days grow brighter, be gentle with yourself, and a smile will be enough"?
Is the text on the wax seal rendered with an embossed effect on a red stamped background with irregular edges?
Is the embossed text located on a wax seal closure?
Is the spelling of the text on the hanging tag exactly "Divide your time in two: study + rest = longer perseverance and a steadier heart"?
Is the hanging tag text rendered as neat printed text?
Is the text on a small hanging tag dangling next to paper-cut flowers?
Is the spelling of the text on the back of the envelope exactly "When you receive this, please send a short reply to let me know you arrived home safely; don't keep me waiting"?
Is this line of small text positioned on the back of the envelope?
Is the spelling of the text on the sticky note exactly "Today's small wish: eat well, sleep well, don't fret over little things or stay up late; tomorrow try again"?
Is the sticky note text rendered in a rounded handwriting style?
Is the sticky note positioned in the lower left corner?
Is the spelling of the text on the faux postage stamp exactly "Thank you for appearing and making an ordinary day worth remembering and revisiting"?
Does the faux postage stamp sticker contain only text with no images?
Is the faux postage stamp sticker positioned in the lower right corner?
Does the image exhibit a multi-layered paper-cut three-dimensional greeting card style with visible handmade paper fibers?

Are the text characters free from structural defects like missing strokes, merged letters, or garbled symbols?
